```stances
{"id":"i1","stance":"support","rationale":"Neither the original code nor the proposed Lua script contains a remove (SREM) operation, meaning both effectively cap total distinct users over the TTL window rather than currently active concurrent users."}
{"id":"i3","stance":"support","rationale":"Because SET_KEY is assigned its expiration before TTL_KEY is set, TTL_KEY will expire slightly later, and any SADD occurring in that short window will falsely skip the expiration block."}
{"id":"i4","stance":"support","rationale":"The Lua script explicitly returns 1 to allow existing members before checking the size limit, whereas the original code evaluated size and rejected requests even for returning users, representing an undocumented behavioral shift."}
{"id":"i5","stance":"support","rationale":"The five operations only occur when adding a new user and the TTL marker does not exist; returning users require two operations, and new users with an existing marker require three."}
{"id":"i6","stance":"support","rationale":"The missing TTL is not a permanent leak, as the very next new user will evaluate hasKey(TTL_KEY) as false and consequently re-apply the expiration to the set."}
```
